title: Pillars of Umrah
description: Pillars are the core actions on which Umrah is built, and it is not valid without performing all of them.

intro.title: What is the difference between a Pillar, a Duty, and a Sunnah?
intro.rukn_body: What Umrah is not valid without, and whoever leaves it remains in their state of Ihram until they perform it.
intro.wajib_body: What must be done; leaving it is a sin and requires a sacrifice (dam) according to the majority of scholars, but the Umrah remains valid.
intro.sunnah_body: What is recommended to do following the Prophet ﷺ, it is not binding, and leaving it does not entail anything.

labels.mustahabbSub: Rewards are given for doing it following the Prophet ﷺ, and leaving it is not a sin.
labels.makroohSub: Matters recommended to avoid because they reduce reward or contradict the completeness of worship.

conclusion.verse: “And complete the Hajj and 'Umrah for Allah.”

rukn1.title: Ihram
rukn1.definition: The intention of entering the state of devotion, which is the pillar with which Umrah begins. Umrah is not valid except with Ihram from the designated Miqat.
rukn1.ruling: Pillar — Umrah is not valid without it. Whoever does not assume Ihram, their Umrah is not initiated at all.
details: Ihram is initiated by intention. When a person intends to enter Umrah and utters it, they enter the state of devotion.
The pilgrim assumes Ihram from the Miqat, and it is not permissible to cross the Miqat without Ihram.
It is recommended to take a bath and wear two white pieces of cloth (Izhar and Rida) for men before Ihram.
Women assume Ihram in their regular covering clothes, avoiding the niqab (face veil) and gloves.
Talbiyah begins immediately after Ihram and continues until starting the Tawaf.
mustahabb: Bathing before Ihram — a stressed Sunnah even if not in a state of ritual impurity.
Wearing a clean white waist-wrapper and upper sheet for men.
Applying perfume to the body (not the clothes) for men before Ihram; if trace remains after, there is no harm.
Facing the Qiblah when reciting Talbiyah if possible.
Raising the voice with Talbiyah for men in a moderate manner.
Stipulation (Ishtirat) for those who fear an obstacle: 'If an obstacle blocks me, then my place of exit is where I am blocked.'
Reciting Talbiyah abundantly on the way to the Grand Mosque.
makrooh: Delaying Talbiyah after the intention of Ihram without excuse.
Being preoccupied with phone and conversation immediately after Ihram before starting Talbiyah.
A woman raising her voice with Talbiyah in front of non-mahram men.
Stipulation without need, as it is a concession not recommended for a healthy person.
Applying perfume to the Ihram sheets after intending Ihram.
rukn1.note: Ihram is the intention, not just wearing the two sheets of cloth. Whoever wears the waist-wrapper and upper sheet without intending to enter the state of devotion has not assumed Ihram.

rukn2.title: Tawaf
rukn2.definition: Circumambulating the Kaaba seven complete times, starting from the Black Stone and ending at it, keeping the Kaaba to the left of the circumambulator at all times.
rukn2.ruling: Pillar — Umrah is not valid without it. Whoever leaves Tawaf, their Umrah is not complete and they do not exit the state of Ihram until they perform it.
details: Each round begins from the alignment of the Black Stone, which is touched or pointed to with Takbeer.
The Kaaba must remain to the left of the circumambulator in all rounds.
Tawaf comprises seven complete rounds, and circumambulating inside Hijr Ismail is not valid.
Purity from major and minor impurities is required for Tawaf.
Idtiba' (exposing the right shoulder) and Ramal (jogging) are Sunnah for men in the Tawaf of Umrah only.
mustahabb: Idtiba' throughout the Tawaf for men — exposing the right shoulder by putting the upper sheet under the right armpit.
Ramal in the first three rounds for men — jogging with short, quick steps.
Touching the Black Stone or pointing to it with Takbeer at the beginning of each round.
Touching the Yamani Corner (Rukn Al-Yamani) with the right hand in each round if possible without causing harm.
Reciting 'Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanah...' between the Yamani Corner and the Black Stone.
Increasing remembrance (dhikr), supplication (dua), and recitation of the Quran throughout Tawaf.
Humility, tranquility, and not being preoccupied with worldly matters.
makrooh: Raising the voice in collective supplication behind a leader, which disturbs other circumambulators.
Crowding and hurting people to reach the Black Stone or the Yamani Corner.
Touching all corners of the Kaaba or rubbing its walls — the Sunnah is to touch the Black Stone and the Yamani Corner only.
Being preoccupied with worldly talk during Tawaf.
Pointing to the Yamani Corner when unable to touch it — the Sunnah is not to point to it.
Running in all rounds — Ramal is in the first three rounds only.
rukn2.note: Kissing the hand after pointing to the Black Stone was not established from the Prophet ﷺ; only pointing with Takbeer is Sunnah.

rukn3.title: Sa'i
rukn3.definition: Walking seven times between the hills of Safa and Marwah, starting from Safa and ending at Marwah.
rukn3.ruling: Pillar — Umrah is not valid without it. Whoever leaves Sa'i, their rituals are not complete and they do not exit Ihram until they perform it.
details: Sa'i consists of seven rounds: going from Safa to Marwah is one round, and returning is another.
Sa'i starts at Safa and ends at Marwah in the seventh round.
It is recommended to ascend Safa and Marwah at each round if possible.
Ritual purity is recommended for Sa'i, but it is not a condition for its validity.
Jogging between the two green posts is Sunnah for men, not women.
mustahabb: Reciting the verse 'Innas-Safa wal-Marwata min sha'a'irillah' when ascending Safa.
Facing the Qiblah on Safa and Marwah and repeating remembrance and supplication three times.
Jogging between the two green posts for men only.
Increasing remembrance, supplication, and reciting what is easy of the Quran.
Ritual purity — recommended, though Sa'i is valid without it.
Ascending Safa and Marwah in every round if possible.
makrooh: Women jogging between the green posts — the Sunnah is to walk normally.
Running throughout the entire path — the Sunnah is to jog between the green posts only.
Starting at Marwah before Safa — the Sunnah is to start at Safa.
Being preoccupied with conversation and leaving remembrance and supplication.
Restricting oneself to a specific dua for each round — this was not established from the Prophet ﷺ.
rukn3.note: Sa'i is valid without ritual purity according to the majority of scholars, though purity is better and more complete.

rukn4.title: Shaving or Cutting
rukn4.definition: Shaving all head hair or cutting it short after Sa'i, which completes the Umrah and releases the pilgrim from Ihram.
rukn4.ruling: Duty (Wajib) according to the majority of jurists — though some consider it a pillar. Whoever leaves it does not exit Ihram.
details: For men: Shaving (Halq) is better, and cutting short (Taqseer) is sufficient. It must cover the entire head.
For women: They clip from the ends of their hair approximately a finger-joint's length, and do not shave their heads.
If Hajj is near and you want to perform Hajj, cutting short is better so you can shave in Hajj.
It is not sufficient to cut a few hairs from the front of the head only.
Upon completing shaving or cutting, the pilgrim exits the state of Ihram and all prohibitions are lifted.
mustahabb: Complete shaving for men unless they want to perform Hajj soon.
Hastening to shave or cut immediately after completing Sa'i.
Supplication after shaving and praising Allah for completing the ritual.
makrooh: Cutting just a few hairs from the front of the head.
Cutting from one side only without the rest of the head.
Exiting Ihram or changing clothes before shaving or cutting.
Women shaving their heads.
rukn4.note: By completing shaving or cutting, your Umrah is completed and you exit Ihram. We ask Allah to accept.
